name: Big Sticks Indoor Golf
about: Big Sticks Indoor Golf is a year-round golf training, practice, and instruction facility founded in 2004. Located minutes from Boston, it offers professional PGA lessons and state-of-the-art technology including aboutGolf simulators, Foresight Sports launch monitors, and JC Video swing analysis systems. The facility focuses on accuracy and improvement, providing a professional environment for golfers of all levels to practice with precise data, including detailed club and ball tracking metrics.
why_golfers_like_it: Tour-level, PGA-endorsed technology for precise swing and ball data
Year-round practice with no weather limitations
Personalized PGA instruction with detailed video swing analysis
Focus on improvement over entertainment with accurate, reliable data
Flexible booking options including hourly bays and lesson packages

food_and_drink.notes: No food service offered; nearby restaurants available for takeout/delivery
No alcohol service provided; BYOB is not mentioned

simulator_hardware.notes: PGA-endorsed with GSP software and 1,000+ courses
Camera-based and infrared technology for precise ball and club data
Overhead-mounted launch monitor for simultaneous left/right-handed play

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, golfers must bring their own clubs to practice at Big Sticks Indoor Golf.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: No, Big Sticks does not allow outside food or drinks. The facility focuses entirely on golf practice and does not offer food service.

faq.question: How much time is needed for 18 holes on a simulator?
faq.answer: One player typically requires one hour to play 18 holes; two players should allow two hours, and so on.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Big Sticks offers private lessons for kids ages 7 and up, making it a great place for junior golfers to learn and play.
